description: End-to-end retraction and republication flow (RFC-ACDP-0013 §6–§8). A producer retracts an active public context via POST /contexts/{ctx_id}/retract with a signed lifecycle event; the body remains retrievable (mark-not-delete), status becomes 'retracted' (dominating superseded/expired per §7.2), search excludes it by default, and a later /republish reverses the retraction while the append-only event history retains both events.

input.precondition: The sig-001 golden context, published and active on a registry advertising acdp-registry-lifecycle (and, for scenarios D/E, acdp-registry-discovery). ctx_id: acdp://registry.example.com/12345678-1234-4321-8123-123456781234; agent_id: did:web:agents.example.com:test-producer.

event.reason: underlying data source found to be fabricated

signature.value: <Ed25519 over the ASCII bytes of 'sha256:<hex(SHA-256(JCS(event minus signature)))>' — RFC-ACDP-0013 §5; verified by the registry through the full RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11 pipeline>

scenarios.name: A — retract succeeds and appends a signed event

expected.behavior: Registry verifies the event signature (§5), checks actor == body.agent_id (§6 step 3), validates the transition (not currently retracted, §6 step 4), appends the event atomically, and returns the full-retrieval envelope: registry_state.status == 'retracted', registry_state.lifecycle_events == [the submitted event, verbatim including its signature].

GET /contexts/{ctx_id} returns HTTP 200 with the byte-identical body, status 'retracted', and the lifecycle_events array. GET /contexts/{ctx_id}/body is entirely unaffected: bare signed body, unchanged bytes, unchanged cache headers (RFC-ACDP-0013 §8.1; docs/non-goals.md §5/§13). The body's content_hash and producer signature still verify — retraction never touches a body byte.

scenarios.name: C — double retract is an invalid transition

expected.behavior: A second POST /retract (fresh event_id, valid signature) while the context is already retracted MUST be rejected with invalid_lifecycle_transition (HTTP 409) and MUST NOT append an event (RFC-ACDP-0013 §6 step 4 — strict retracted/republished alternation).

scenarios.name: D — default search excludes retracted contexts

expected.behavior: GET /contexts/search?q=golden (status filter defaulting to 'active', RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.1) MUST NOT include the retracted context in matches[] nor count it in total_estimate. It also MUST NOT match status=superseded or status=expired even where those facts hold (§7.2 precedence applies to the filter).

scenarios.name: E — explicit status=retracted search returns it

expected.behavior: GET /contexts/search?q=golden&status=retracted returns the context (visibility scoping unchanged, RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.5.5 — retraction never widens an audience).

scenarios.name: F — republish reverses, history is append-only

expected.behavior: POST /contexts/{ctx_id}/republish with a signed event (event_type 'republished', fresh event_id) returns 200; status re-derives per RFC-ACDP-0004 §4 (here: 'active'); lifecycle_events now holds BOTH events in order — the retraction is reversed in effect but never erased from history (RFC-ACDP-0013 §4.1, §7.1). The default search match returns.

notes: Registries not advertising acdp-registry-lifecycle MUST return not_implemented (501) on both endpoints and never emit lifecycle_events or 'retracted' (RFC-ACDP-0013 §6). Pre-0.3.0 consumers treat 'retracted' as an unknown status per RFC-ACDP-0004 §4.1 (tolerate, treat as active, log) — the search-default exclusion in scenario D is registry-side and protects them on the discovery path regardless.
